Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day, which means she uses [number] ounces per week. A leap year has 366 days, so she washes her hair [number] times in that period. To find the total amount of shampoo she needs, multiply the number of washes by 1/4 ounce. Once the total is known, divide it by 14 to determine how many bottles she needs to buy. This calculation ensures she has enough shampoo for the entire year without running out.

If the total shampoo required exceeds the size of one bottle, Brittany should round up to the next whole number to ensure she has enough. For example, if she needs 15 ounces and each bottle contains 14 ounces, she would need [number] bottles. This approach guarantees she doesn’t have to purchase an additional bottle mid-year. By considering both the frequency of use and the size of the bottles, she can plan her purchases efficiently.
